name: Hjalmar
description: Hjalmar was a legendary Swedish Viking hero, primarily known from the Hervarar saga ok Heiðreks and other Norse sagas. He was renowned for his strength, courage, and fierce warrior spirit. Hjalmar was a contemporary and close companion of the berserker Arngrim's twelve sons, particularly Angantyr. His most famous exploit involves a single combat duel on Samsø against Angantyr, the strongest of Arngrim's sons. This duel was prompted by a dispute over who was the greater warrior and who deserved the hand of Ingeborg, the princess of Sweden. While Hjalmar and his men initially held their own, the superior numbers of Angantyr's brothers eventually overwhelmed them. Hjalmar was mortally wounded by Angantyr during the battle. He lived long enough to return to Uppsala and bid farewell to Ingeborg. His death is a tragic tale of honor, friendship, and the cost of glory. Hjalmar's legacy lives on as a symbol of Viking bravery and the complexities of warrior culture. His story highlights the importance of loyalty, the allure of battle, and the somber reality of death in the Viking age. He represents the ideal of a noble warrior, even in defeat.
subtitle: Viking hero, warrior, and tragic figure.
achievements: Engaged in single combat with Angantyr
Won the respect of his enemies
Died a heroic death in battle

keyMyths.title: Duel on Samsø
keyMyths.description: Hjalmar fought a duel with Angantyr on Samsø to decide who was the greater warrior. Hjalmar was mortally wounded in the battle and ultimately died from his injuries.

keyMyths.title: Hjalmar and Ingeborg
keyMyths.description: Hjalmar sought the hand of Ingeborg, a Swedish princess. His quest for her hand led to the conflict with Angantyr and his brothers.

keyMyths.title: Death and Farewell
keyMyths.description: After being mortally wounded, Hjalmar returned to Uppsala to bid farewell to Ingeborg. His death is depicted as a tragic end to a brave warrior.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: Primary source for the story of Hjalmar and Angantyr.
sources.text: Primary source for the story of Hjalmar and Angantyr.
